query: best practices for API rate limiting

results.title: API Rate Limiting: Strategies and Best Practices
results.snippet: Rate limiting protects your API from abuse and ensures fair usage. Common strategies include token bucket, leaky bucket, and fixed window counters. This guide covers implementation patterns for each approach.

results.title: Implementing Rate Limiting with Redis
results.snippet: Redis sorted sets provide an efficient data structure for sliding window rate limiting. This tutorial walks through a production-ready implementation with sub-millisecond overhead.

results.title: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests — Handling Rate Limit Responses
results.snippet: When your API returns 429, clients should respect the Retry-After header and implement exponential backoff. This article explains how to communicate rate limit status clearly to API consumers.

results.title: Asyncio in Python: A Complete Guide
results.snippet: Python's asyncio library enables concurrent I/O-bound operations without threads. Learn how to use async/await syntax, event loops, and coroutines to write efficient asynchronous code.

results.title: Understanding Python Coroutines
results.snippet: Coroutines are the building blocks of async Python. This reference covers the difference between coroutines, tasks, and futures, and explains how the event loop schedules execution.

results.title: Async Database Access with SQLAlchemy
results.snippet: SQLAlchemy 1.4+ supports async sessions for non-blocking database access. This tutorial demonstrates how to integrate async SQLAlchemy with FastAPI for high-throughput applications.

results.title: Blue-Green Deployments for ML Models
results.snippet: Blue-green deployment allows you to switch traffic between model versions with zero downtime. This pattern is especially useful for A/B testing new model versions against production baselines.

results.title: Canary Releases for Machine Learning
results.snippet: Canary releases gradually shift traffic to a new model version, allowing you to monitor performance metrics before full rollout. Learn how to implement canary deployments with feature flags.

results.title: Model Versioning and Registry Best Practices
results.snippet: A model registry provides a central store for tracking model versions, their metadata, and deployment status. This guide covers how to structure your registry for reproducibility and auditability.

query: how to write effective unit tests

results.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
results.snippet: Effective unit tests are fast, isolated, and deterministic. The AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert) provides a clear structure for writing tests that are easy to read and maintain.

results.title: Test-Driven Development in Practice
results.snippet: TDD involves writing tests before implementation, which forces you to think about the interface before the internals. This guide walks through a real-world example using the red-green-refactor cycle.

results.title: Mocking and Stubbing in Python Tests
results.snippet: Python's unittest.mock library provides tools for replacing dependencies in tests. Learn when to use mocks versus stubs, and how to avoid over-mocking that leads to brittle tests.

results.title: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler Guide
results.snippet: The Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods based on CPU utilization or custom metrics. This guide covers HPA configuration, metrics server setup, and scaling behavior tuning.

results.title: Vertical Pod Autoscaler Best Practices
results.snippet: VPA adjusts CPU and memory requests for pods based on historical usage. Learn how to configure VPA update modes and avoid common pitfalls like resource request oscillation.

results.title: Custom Metrics Autoscaling with KEDA
results.snippet: KEDA enables autoscaling based on external event sources like message queue depth or database row counts. This tutorial shows how to configure KEDA scalers for common workloads.

results.title: Monitoring Data Pipelines with Prometheus
results.snippet: Instrumenting data pipelines with Prometheus metrics allows you to track throughput, latency, and error rates. This guide covers key metrics to expose and how to set up meaningful alerts.

results.title: Data Quality Checks in ETL Pipelines
results.snippet: Automated data quality checks catch schema drift, null value anomalies, and statistical outliers before they reach downstream consumers. Learn how to integrate quality gates into your pipeline.

results.title: Alerting on Pipeline SLA Breaches
results.snippet: SLA-based alerting notifies on-call engineers when pipeline jobs exceed their expected completion time. This article explains how to define SLOs for batch jobs and configure deadline-based alerts.

results.title: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow
results.snippet: The authorization code flow is the most secure OAuth 2.0 grant type for server-side applications. It involves a redirect to the authorization server, an authorization code exchange, and token issuance.

results.title: PKCE Extension for Public Clients
results.snippet: PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) prevents authorization code interception attacks in mobile and single-page applications. This guide explains how to implement PKCE in your OAuth 2.0 flow.

results.title: Refresh Token Rotation Best Practices
results.snippet: Refresh token rotation issues a new refresh token with each access token renewal, limiting the window of exposure if a refresh token is compromised. Learn how to implement rotation safely.

results.title: Understanding EXPLAIN ANALYZE Output
results.snippet: EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows the actual execution plan and timing for a query. Learning to read the output — including seq scans, index scans, and join types — is essential for query optimization.

results.title: Index Types in PostgreSQL
results.snippet: PostgreSQL supports B-tree, Hash, GIN, GiST, and BRIN indexes. Choosing the right index type for your access patterns can dramatically reduce query time and storage overhead.

results.title: Partitioning Large Tables in PostgreSQL
results.snippet: Table partitioning splits large tables into smaller physical segments, improving query performance through partition pruning. This guide covers range, list, and hash partitioning strategies.

results.title: Redux vs Zustand vs Jotai: A 2024 Comparison
results.snippet: The React state management landscape has evolved significantly. This comparison covers bundle size, boilerplate, DevTools support, and performance characteristics of the leading libraries.

results.title: When to Use React Context vs External State
results.snippet: React Context is suitable for low-frequency updates like theme and locale. For high-frequency state changes, external libraries like Zustand or Redux avoid unnecessary re-renders.

results.title: Server State Management with TanStack Query
results.snippet: TanStack Query handles server state — caching, background refetching, and synchronization — separately from client state. This guide explains how to structure your application around this distinction.

results.title: Synchronous vs Asynchronous Microservice Communication
results.snippet: Synchronous communication (REST, gRPC) is simpler but creates temporal coupling. Asynchronous messaging (Kafka, RabbitMQ) improves resilience but adds operational complexity. This guide helps you choose.

results.title: The Saga Pattern for Distributed Transactions
results.snippet: The Saga pattern manages distributed transactions across microservices using a sequence of local transactions with compensating actions for rollback. Learn choreography vs orchestration approaches.

results.title: Service Mesh with Istio: Traffic Management
results.snippet: Istio's service mesh provides traffic management, observability, and security for microservices without application code changes. This tutorial covers virtual services, destination rules, and circuit breaking.
